hero.title: One pane of glass for your firewall fleet.
hero.subtitle: SNMP, sFlow, NetFlow/IPFIX, syslog, traps, and config-drift monitoring across every major firewall platform — and any SNMP device. Multi-user with roles and 2FA. Self-hosted. One container.

why.title: Built firewall-first.
why.intro: General network monitoring tools give you a wall of generic OID graphs. Firewall-Mon is built specifically for firewalls.
why.card1_desc: Native profiles across many firewall platforms read CPU, sessions, VPN tunnels, HA states, and SPU/ASIC metrics out-of-the-box. Zero MIB wrestling.
why.card2_desc: Every config change is classified by risk—so a rule opened to ANY surfaces before it reaches production, not during your next audit.
why.card3_desc: Remote site probes push metrics over outbound HTTPS. No inbound ports need to be exposed, keeping your branches completely locked down.

demo.title: The Live Operations Dashboard
demo.intro: Experience the interface network security engineers use to monitor a distributed firewall fleet in real-time.
demo.initiating_poller_cycle: Initiating global poller cycle for device fleet.
demo.poller_cycle_complete_msg: Poller cycle complete for device fleet.

drift.title: Risk-Classified Config Drift
drift.intro: Every config modification is normalized to a vendor-neutral model. Our security classification system parses each diff and flags security vulnerabilities.
drift.medium_tab_desc: IPsec VPN Proposal DH Group weakened

sflow.title: Multi-Protocol Flow Analytics
sflow.intro: Ingest sFlow v5, NetFlow v5/v9, and IPFIX side by side — with content-based version dispatch, per-vendor template decoding, and denied-flow visibility from firewall events. Triage spikes on raw conversation-level detail.
sflow.alert_text: Anomalous outbound traffic on Port 4444 from host 10.200.5.88 to 185.112.145.4 (known Metasploit port). A NetFlow denied-flow event (firewall_event=denied) from the same external IP correlates an inbound Telnet probe. Rule matching: Suspicious_Shell_Outbound.

access.title: Multi-user, with real roles.
access.intro: Firewall-Mon is no longer a single-admin tool. Give your team scoped access, protect logins with two-factor authentication, and automate with API tokens — all enforced server-side.
access.card1_desc: Three roles — admin, operator, and viewer — enforced on every route, not just hidden in the UI. Operators run day-to-day changes; viewers get read-only; only admins touch users, credentials, and settings.
access.card2_desc: Opt-in TOTP 2FA (RFC 6238) with single-use recovery codes and a guided enrollment wizard. Secrets are encrypted at rest; enabling or disabling 2FA revokes every live session instantly.
access.card3_desc: Issue read or read/write tokens for automation and service accounts. Token privileges follow the owner's current role, and disabling or deleting an account instantly revokes its tokens.

alerting.title: Alerts that behave like a NOC.
alerting.intro: Cut the noise and route the signal. Firewall-Mon groups incidents, escalates through tiered on-call steps, and baselines each device against its own history.
alerting.sg1_desc: When a device goes offline, its CPU, interface, and VPN alerts attach to a single incident and stay muted — so you get one story and one recovery summary, not a pager storm.
alerting.sg2_desc: Define tiered steps per policy — 5 min, 15 min, 60 min — each routed to exactly its own channels and recipients. Unacknowledged alerts climb the chain automatically.
alerting.sg3_desc: Clear-band hysteresis stops fire/recover flapping, and adaptive z-score baselining alerts when a metric strays from the device's own trailing-24h normal — not a static guess.
alerting.sg4_desc: Alerts that fire and resolve repeatedly are tagged and silenced automatically, then resume on their own once the pattern settles — no manual muting, no missed real events.
alerting.integrations_desc: Deliver to the channels your team already lives in. PagerDuty, Opsgenie, and Teams are stateful — recovery events resolve the incident they opened. Webhooks are HMAC-signed.
alerting.mttr_note: Executive reports track MTTA / MTTR and your noisiest alerts over a rolling 30 days.

probe.title: Distributed Probe-Relay
probe.intro: Deploy local collectors at remote sites to sync telemetry behind CGNAT over outbound HTTPS, maintaining data parity on shaky uplinks.
probe.sg1_desc: Every packet batch is stamped with a unique, UUID-based Header. Retries on packet drops are validated at rest, preventing duplicated log analysis or traffic spikes.
probe.sg2_desc: If a remote probe and the central collector undergo a schema mismatch, `fwmon-api` flags HTTP 426 Upgrade Required. The probe's internal disk queue holds, locking your logs until safe migration completes.
probe.sg3_desc: Probe authentication keys and device passwords are hashed at rest using AES-256-GCM. Decryption verification is performed on start; pollers fail-fast immediately on key modification.

features.title: Production-grade monitoring.
features.intro: Built for speed, compliance posture, and any firewall you run. Tap a card for the detail.
features.card1_desc: Any SNMP-capable firewall, with native per-platform OID profiles.
features.card2_desc: Surface a rule opened to ANY before it ships.
features.card3_desc: sFlow, NetFlow v5/v9 & IPFIX — with denied-flow visibility. No PCAP.
features.card4_desc: Branch firewalls behind CGNAT, polled through one outbound HTTPS.
features.card6_desc: AES-256-GCM secrets, fail-fast keys, and an append-only audit log.
features.card7_desc: RBAC, TOTP two-factor with recovery codes, and scoped API tokens.
features.card8_desc: Incident grouping, escalation chains, PagerDuty, Opsgenie & Teams.
features.card1_detail: Native OID profiles for every major firewall platform, plus a standards-only generic profile, mean CPU, sessions, VPN tunnels, HA state, and interface health arrive on day one — no MIB hunting, no per-device templates to hand-build.
features.card2_detail: Every configuration snapshot is normalized and compared, and each change is scored by risk. A rule opened to ANY, a disabled log, or a weakened policy is flagged the moment it appears — so you catch it before it ships, not during the next audit.
features.card3_detail: sFlow v5, NetFlow v5/v9, and IPFIX are ingested side by side with content-based version dispatch and per-platform template decoding. Denied-flow visibility comes straight from firewall events, so you can triage a spike down to the conversation without a packet capture.
features.card4_detail: Remote and branch sites run a lightweight probe that pushes metrics out over a single outbound HTTPS connection. Nothing inbound is exposed, so firewalls behind CGNAT or strict egress rules are monitored without opening a port.
features.card5_detail: One docker compose up -d brings up the full stack with PostgreSQL 16 bundled. No external database to provision and no message broker to babysit — it is self-hosted and running in well under a minute.
features.card6_detail: SNMP communities, SSH credentials, TOTP secrets, and notification keys are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM. Pollers fail fast if a secret cannot be decrypted, and every administrative action lands in an append-only audit trail.
features.card7_detail: Admin, operator, and viewer roles are enforced server-side, with scoped API tokens for automation. Opt-in TOTP two-factor with recovery codes protects every account without a third-party identity provider.
features.card8_detail: Alerts are grouped into incidents with escalation chains, flap suppression, and z-score baselining to cut noise. Route them to PagerDuty, Opsgenie, or Microsoft Teams, and track MTTA/MTTR to see how fast you actually respond.

reports.title: Email reports, redesigned.
reports.intro: Newly styled daily digests featuring Plus Jakarta Sans and modern dark gradients—crafted to be entirely compatible with Gmail and Outlook.

security.title: Continuously audited. Zero open.
security.intro: Firewall-Mon is independently engineering-audited and re-checked in CI on every build. Encryption, access control, and audit logging are verified continuously — open findings stay at zero.
security.item1_desc: SNMP communities, SSH credentials, TOTP secrets, and every notification key — Slack, PagerDuty, Opsgenie, and webhooks — are encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM.
security.item2_desc: Pollers and receivers crash immediately on initialization if database encryption credentials fail to decrypt secrets. Prevents stale polling states.
security.item3_desc: All administrative actions, login events, and config backups generate immutable logs written directly to standard output.

quickstart.title: 30 seconds to first poll.
quickstart.intro: Deploy the application locally or in production with a single command.
quickstart.docker_desc: Pull the published image from Docker Hub and bring up the full stack — PostgreSQL 16 is bundled — with one command:
quickstart.systemd_desc: Run Firewall-Mon natively as a systemd daemon. Compile the single binary using Go 1.22+:
quickstart.relay_desc: Run the lightweight Firewall-Collector on remote servers behind branch firewalls to relay SNMP data to the main server. The image is hosted on Docker Hub:

footer.brand_desc: Vendor-agnostic firewall monitoring system built for speed, security audits, and complex topology traversal.
